CALL FOR PROPOSALS: independent audit of the Council’s 2024 financial report
ACT Tanzania
News
28 November 2025
The Agricultural Council of Tanzania (ACT) invites proposals from qualified and reputable audit firms to conduct an independent audit of the Council’s 2024 financial report. The purpose of this audit is to enhance transparency, ensure compliance with applicable laws and regulations, and strengthen accountability to our stakeholders.
Interested and eligible audit firms are encouraged to submit their proposals in accordance with the guidelines provided in the attached Call for Proposals.
Application Timeline:
The submission window is open for 7 days (one week) starting from today. Late submissions will not be considered.
We look forward to receiving proposals from capable firms committed to delivering high-quality, professional audit services.
Issued by:
Agricultural Council of Tanzania (ACT)
CALL FOR PROPOSAL ACT

RICE PROCESSING FACILITY BY ACT-BFC
ACT Tanzania
News
20 November 2025
As part of its ongoing commitment to strengthen the rice value chain, ACT, with generous support from the Beyond Farming Collective (BFC),has successfully established a modern rice processing facility in Mkindo Village, Mvomero District,Morogoro Region. The initiative aims toempower local rice farmers through improved processing efficiency, enhanced product quality, and better market returns.

extracted_texts.CALL-FOR-PROPOSAL-ACT.pdf:
CALL FOR PROPOSAL

                 AUDIT OF AGRICULTURAL COUNCIL OF TANZANIA.



The Agricultural Council of Tanzania is an apex organization for private sector stakeholders in
agriculture (crop, livestock, fisheries and forestry etc.) uniting and representing them to safeguard
their interests. Its core function is lobbying and advocacy for better business environment of its
members and the general agricultural community. It was established in 1999 but became
operationally active since 2005 when the Secretariat (for overseeing activities of the organization
on daily basis) was installed.


The Agricultural Council of Tanzania (ACT) invites proposals from qualified and reputable audit
firms to conduct an independent audit of the Council’s 2024 financial report. The purpose of this
audit is to enhance transparency, ensure compliance with applicable laws and regulations, and
strengthen accountability to our stakeholders.


General scope of work

The auditor shall audit Financial Statements and express an audit opinion(s) on whether the
financial statements of the present fairly, in all material respects, the financial position of the
Organization, and on funds received and expenditures incurred for all projects implemented by
ACT on the relevant accounting period in conformity with the International Financial Reporting
Standards.


REQUIREMENTS
An interested organization(s) shall be required to do the following:
1. Submit Company/organization profile accompanied by copies of Certificate of
Registration/Incorporation & Tax Identification Number (TIN), CVs of technical staff who will
be involved in this assignment;
2. Technical proposal describing how the assignment will be conducted;
3. Detailed financial proposal highlighting all costs expected to be incurred;
4. The application should be submitted electronically to ACT at or before Friday, 5th
November, 2025. Late submission shall not be accepted for evaluation irrespective of the
circumstances.
 5. Submission should be made through the following e-mail address; act@actanzania.or.tz; and
copied to prosperdionis@gmail.com neemajames7@gmail.com
You are kindly requested to acknowledge receipt of this call for proposal during submission of
your proposal.

                                     Executive Director,
                             Agricultural Council of Tanzania,
                         Mazwile Street near TRA Office-Mwenge,
                             Plot no 275, Block 4, House no 12,
                          P.o.Box 14130, Dar es salaam, Tanzania.
extracted_texts.Audit-ToR-ACT.pdf:
P. O. Box 14130 Dar es Salaam;
                         Tel: +255 22-2124851 Fax: +255 22 2128032
                    Email: act@actanzania.or.tz ; Web: www.actanzania.or.tz

                             Audit Terms of Reference (TOR)
                       Agricultural council of Tanzania
1. Background
Agricultural Council of Tanzania is an apex organization for private sector stakeholders in
agriculture (crop, livestock, fisheries and forestry etc.) uniting and representing them to
safeguard their interests. Its core function is lobbying and advocacy for better business
environment of its members and the general agricultural community. It was established in 1999
but became operationally active since 2005 when the Secretariat (for overseeing activities of the
organization on daily basis) was installed.


2. Objectives and Standards
The objective of the audit is to enable the auditor to express an audit opinion(s) on whether the
Financial statements of the project present fairly, in all material respects, the financial position of
the project, and on funds received and expenditures incurred for the relevant accounting period
in conformity with the International Financial Reporting Standards. The auditor shall conduct this
audit in accordance with the International Auditing Standards.



3. Preparation of the Financial statements

The responsibility for the preparation of financial statements including adequate disclosure is
that of Agricultural Council of Tanzania. The Agricultural Council of Tanzania is also responsible
for the selection and application of accounting policies, procedures, guidelines and systems that
entertain sound and professional financial management.

The auditor is responsible for forming and expressing opinions on the financial statements. The
auditor would carry out the audit of the project in accordance with the International Standards
on Auditing (ISA), as promulgated by the International Federation of Accountants (IFAC). As part
 of the audit process, the auditor may request from the implementing agency written
confirmation concerning representations made in connection with the audit




4. Scope of the Audit
The annual financial statements of ACT shall be audited in accordance with International
Standards of Auditing (ISA), promulgated by the International Federation of Accountants (IFAC),
and will include such tests and auditing procedures as the auditor will consider necessary under
the circumstances. The auditor shall comply with all ISAs relevant to the audit and special
attention shall be provided on:

       a) ISA 200 (Overall Objectives of the Independent auditor and the conduct of an audit in
          accordance with international standards on auditing) paragraph 18 and 20.
       b) ISA 800 (Special Considerations audits of single financial statements and specific
          elements, accounts or items of financial statements”).

       c) Fraud and Corruption: Consider the risks of material misstatements in the financial
          statements due to fraud as required by ISA 240: The Auditor’s Responsibility to
          Consider Fraud in an Audit of Financial Statements. The auditor is required to identify
          and assess these risks (of material misstatement of the financial statements) due to
          fraud, obtain sufficient appropriate audit evidence about the assessed risks; and
          respond appropriately to identified or suspected fraud;


       d) Laws and Regulations: In designing and performing audit procedures, evaluating and
          reporting the results, consider that noncompliance by the implementing agency with
          laws and regulations may materially affect the financial statements as required by ISA
          250: Consideration of Laws and Regulations in an Audit of Financial Statements;


       e) Governance: Communicate audit matters of governance interest arising from the
          audit of financial statements with those charged with governance of an entity as
          required by International Standards on Auditing 260: Communication of Audit
          Matters with those Charged with Governance.


       f) Risks: In order to reduce audit risk to an acceptable low level, determine the overall
          responses to assessed risks at the financial statement level, and design and perform
          further audit procedures to respond to assessed risks at the assertion level as
          required by Internal Standard on Auditing 330: The Auditor’s Procedures in Response
          to Assessed Risks.
 The Audit shall cover a period from 1st January 2024 to 31st December 2024.


5. Deliverables and Time Schedule

The auditor is required to deliver an audit package that includes:

   a. Identification of the total expenses and total income
   b. The subject of the Audit
   c. The financial reporting framework applied
   d. The Auditing standards applied
   e. A statement that the auditor has obtained reasonable assurance about whether the
      financial statements as a whole are free from material misstatement.
   f. Auditors opinion
   g. In addition to the reports, the auditor shall submit a management letter (matters for
      governance attention) which shall contain any findings made during the audit of the
      Project.




6. Requirement for the Auditor
6.1. General Principles
By agreeing these ToR, the auditor confirms meeting at least one of the following conditions:
    • The auditor is a member of a national accounting or auditing body or institution which in
       turn is a member of the International Federation of Accountants (IFAC).
    • The auditor is a member of a national accounting or auditing body or institution.
       Although this organization is not member of the IFAC, the auditor commits to undertake
       the engagement in accordance with the IFAC standards and ethics set out in these ToR.

6.2. Qualifications, Experience, and Team Composition
6.2.1. Qualification and Experience
The auditor will employ staff with appropriate professional qualifications and suitable experience
with IFAC standards and with experience in verifying financial information of entities comparable
in size and complexity to the Recipient. In addition, the audit team as whole should have:

   •    Experience with audits in Tanzania, with IPSAs application as a key area.
   •    Experience with audits of Donor funded projects.
    •   Sufficient knowledge of relevant laws, regulations and rules in the country concerned.
       This includes but is not limited to taxation, social security and labor regulations,
       accounting and reporting.
   •   Fluency in English


6.2.2. Team composition
The team of auditors required for this engagement will be composed of a category 1 auditor who
has the ultimate responsibility for the expenditure verification and a team which is composed of
an appropriate mix of category 2 – 4 auditors.

6.2.3. Categories of staff/experts

Category 1 – (Audit Partner)
A Category 1 expert (audit partner) should be a partner or another person in a position similar to
that of a partner and be a highly qualified expert with relevant professional qualifications who
assumes or has assumed senior and managerial responsibilities in public audit practice. He/she
should be a member of a national or international accounting or auditing body or institution.
He/she must have at least 12 years of professional experience as a professional auditor or
accountant in public audit practice. The audit partner will be the person who will be responsible
for the specific contract and its performance as well as for the report that is issued on behalf of
the firm. He/she has the appropriate authority from a professional, legal or regulatory body and
is authorized to certify accounts by the laws of the country in which the audit firm is registered.

Category 2 – (e.g. Audit Manager)
Audit managers should be qualified experts with a relevant university degree or professional
qualification. They should have at least 6 years of experience as a professional auditor or
accountant in public audit practice including relevant managerial experience of leading audit
teams. He/she should be a member of a national or international accounting or auditing body or
institution.

Category 3 – (e.g. Senior Auditor)
Senior auditors should be qualified experts with a relevant university degree or professional
qualification and at least 3 years professional experience as a professional auditor or accountant
in public audit practice.

Category 4 – (e.g. Assistant Auditor)
Assistant auditors should have a relevant university degree and at least 6 months professional
experience in public audit practice.

6.2.4. Curriculum Vitae (CVs)
The auditor will provide the Recipient with CVs of the staff/experts involved in the engagement.
The CVs will include appropriate details for the purpose of the evaluation of the offer on the
relevant specific experience for this engagement and the qualifying work carried out in the past.
